explanation.title: Text does not have enough contrast against its background to be easy to read
explanation.impact: The rendered accessibility defect can prevent people using assistive technology from completing a task.
explanation.reasoning: The rendered foreground and background colours did not meet the rule.
limitations: Confirm brand-approved colours in the affected state.

basis.criterion: The check uses an axe rule against the rendered accessibility tree and exposes the rule-specific help reference.

remediation.recommendation: Raise the contrast between the text and its background until the ratio is at least 4.5 to 1 for normal text, or 3 to 1 for large text (18 point, or 14 point bold and above). Darken the text, lighten the background, or pick a different colour pair, then re-check the ratio. Readers with low vision, and anyone in bright sunlight or on a dim screen, will be able to read the text.
